mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, exerting downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations. This relationship holds as a persistent negative correlation regardless of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals a severe credit contraction and housing market crash. This threshold breach predicts a sharp decline in consumer confidence and equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1500.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and slowing economic growth (GDPC1) creates a stagflationary environment. This interaction forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy despite weak growth, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and GDPC1 has a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates during the late stages of an economic expansion signal deteriorating labor market health. This specific regime condition predicts negative forward returns as investors anticipate a recessionary earnings slowdown.
failure_condition: UNRATE shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) specifically during late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in the index of industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization. This supply-side strength supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum is negatively correlated with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales exceeding 350 billion dollars indicates strong consumer spending momentum. This threshold activation signals immediate demand strength, predicting positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 350000.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total payrolls (PAYEMS) continue to grow while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises suggests labor force participation issues or statistical noise. This specific interaction pattern predicts positive returns as the labor market remains structurally tight.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE levels has a negative or null effect on forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: High real interest rates, defined as the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) minus inflation (CPIAUCSL), suppress equity valuations. This interaction is particularly potent in high inflation regimes where nominal rates fail to fully offset price increases.
failure_condition: Real rates (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL) are positively correlated with forward returns during high inflation periods.
